name: cultural_heritage_procurement_shortcut_kestrel_tru_loy
premise: A regional manager wants an exception to policy; compliance says any carve-out must be written, time-limited, and auditable. In Cultural Heritage, the situation tagged internally around cultural heritage procurement shortcut kestrel tru loy has converged with local pressure in the Stonehaven corridor. That label is not a full explanation, but it is how crews, counsel, and comms are indexing the case file right now. Frontline managers report that frontline staff are already deviating from policy; silence from the top will be read as tacit approval. A vendor’s root-cause paragraph contradicts your internal postmortem draft; both cannot be true, but neither is fully disprovable yet. Shift change is imminent; handoff cannot proceed without a signed disposition because liability follows the signatory line. The trade is not abstract: it is truth versus loyalty under incomplete information, and whichever value you deprioritize will have defenders who are technically correct about the risk they fear.

agents.name: Fatima Navarro
agents.identity: I am Fatima Navarro, the duty officer with sign-off authority for the next window in Cultural Heritage. I own the signature line that turns a recommendation into an irreversible operational fact for the next phase.
agents.goal: I need a path that does not treat loyalty as disposable collateral every time truth spikes in urgency, but I also cannot pretend we have luxury time we do not have.

agents.name: Darius Vasquez
agents.identity: I am Darius Vasquez, the field counterpart whose constituency will feel the decision first in Cultural Heritage. I am tracking who gets protected last if we choose speed, silence, or a narrow definition of success.
agents.goal: I want explicit guardrails so truth cannot become a blank check that quietly vaporizes loyalty in the paperwork afterward.

choices: I will sequence a conservative rollout: smallest blast radius first, documented exceptions only, and real appeals paths—even if truth advocates call that foot-dragging.
I will push a fast, organization-wide lever because partial fixes may leave the worst harm untouched—even if that compresses protections for loyalty.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Partial evidence is all that will exist before the window closes. Fatima Navarro must choose under truth versus loyalty. What does Fatima Navarro do?
